sentence: וַיִּקְרָא אֱלֹהִים לָרָקִיעַ שָׁמָיִם וַיְהִי־עֶרֶב וַיְהִי־בֹקֶר יוֹם שֵׁנִי

grammatical-notes.וַיִּקְרָא: Waw-consecutive + Verb. Act of legal naming.
grammatical-notes.לָרָקִיעַ: Preposition + Article + Noun 'raqia'. The object being named.
grammatical-notes.יוֹם שֵׁנִי: Noun 'yom' (day) + Ordinal number 'sheni' (second). Anarthrous (no article), signaling a qualitative sequential step.

dictionary-meaning.וַיִּקְרָא: Then Elohim assigned the functional name of — disclosing the sovereign act of defining the expanse's identity (Rule 24).
dictionary-meaning.שָׁמָיִם: The expansive heights of the skies — preserving the topographical definition established in Unit 1.
dictionary-meaning.שֵׁנִי: A second day — disclosing the ordinal nature of the number 'sheni' and the lack of a definite article (Rule 69, 75).

שמים.chosen: the expansive heights of the skies
שמים.reason: Maintains consistency with the topographical disclosure used for the same term in Genesis 1:1.

main-translation: ***Then Elohim assigned the functional name of 'the expansive heights of the skies' to the hammered-out expanse; and there was evening (mixing) and there was morning (discernment), comprising a second day.***

main-translation-explanation.the expansive heights of the skies: Reused the topographical expansion to show the functional identity of the expanse (Rule 21).
main-translation-explanation.comprising a second day: Discloses the ordinal nature of 'sheni' and the lack of a definite article in the Hebrew (Rule 69, 75).

rejected-translations.reason: Adds a definite article not present in the Hebrew; fails to preserve the qualitative sequential nature of the text (Rule 75).
